name: Inspiration Mars
first: First proposed crewed Mars free-return mission (concept, never flew)
description: A 2013 mission concept by Dennis Tito's Inspiration Mars Foundation for a 501-day crewed Mars free-return flyby targeting the 2018 launch window. Two-person crew (a married couple was envisioned for psychological stability) launches on a Falcon Heavy or equivalent into a heliocentric trajectory that swings around Mars without orbit insertion and uses the planet's gravity to slingshot back to Earth. The free-return geometry minimises ∆v — no Mars orbit insertion or trans-Earth burn required — at the cost of a longer transit and a one-shot launch window. The concept did not advance to development; it remains a published reference architecture for crewed cismartian flight.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: Concept launch on Falcon Heavy (or equivalent) into trans-Mars trajectory.

events.label: TMI
events.note: Trans-Mars injection burn — heliocentric Mars-bound orbit.

events.label: MARS FLYBY
events.note: Closest approach to Mars (~100 km altitude in original concept). No orbit insertion — gravity assist sets up Earth-return trajectory.

events.label: EARTH RE-ENTRY
events.note: Return capsule re-enters Earth's atmosphere at ~14.2 km/s — among the fastest crewed re-entries ever proposed.
